title: #Tags vs. Dropdown
excerpt: Hi All.  From what I can see, Tags and Dropdowns look similar.  I could have tags #internal and #external or dropdown internal and external.  Both can show up on a list and be multi-selected.  Is there any guidance when …

posts.author: BruceB (Bruce Baldwin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All. From what I can see, Tags and Dropdowns look similar. I could have tags #internal and #external or dropdown internal and external. Both can show up on a list and be multi-selected. Is there any guidance when to use one over another, pros / cons, functional differences? To me it looks like they convey the same information.

Thanks!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BruceB
I favor dropdown over tags mainly because tags can be somewhat confusing. There are tags that are bound to a board and there are account wide tags. Shareable and private boards have local tags (scope = board) where public / main boards has tags with an account scope. This can become messy specially if you have a tags with the same name in both types of boards. In that scenario the items looks to be tagged the same but they aren’t (because of the different scope).

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @BruceB - I completely agree with @basdebruin on this one. While tags have their place, we always lean towards Dropdown column-types mainly for the reasons Bas mentioned.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @BruceB

It looks like @basdebruin has a great answer regarding the general difference between the uses of the Dropdown Column and the Tags Column!

You can also refer to the articles below to learn more about each column in general:

  • The Dropdown Column

  • The Tags Column

If you have any additional questions for any of us regarding this topic, let us know! Otherwise, remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: evernon9 (Erica Vernon)
posts.cookedHtml:
I find myself wishing there was a mix between the two as I like certain features of each that are not applicable to other.

For example, I like how tags give a quick overview of how many items are tagged for each tag. I also like the ability to search for tags in the global search bar and have each tag more easily identified by color. These features are not available for labels that are a part of a dropdown.

I like how labels in a dropdown can be created before actually applying them to any item so you have a bank of options to choose from even if they are not being used yet. This is not an option for tags.

Ideally, both need to be allowed to use in automations and able to be cleared without individually unchecking every instance. This would be helpful since I would like to use the tag or label “New Client” which will need to be reset each year. I would also like to use a tag or label to indicate a type of client that will not change year over year.
